turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having a persistent cough for about five days now. It started out dry and has become productive with yellowish-green sputum, which is really concerning me.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The cough - it's been constant, especially at night when I'm trying to sleep. It's like a tickle in my throat that just won't go away, and now it's producing this thick, greenish-yellow sputum that's really scaring me.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about five days ago. I think it was a Wednesday when I first noticed it was dry, and by the weekend, it had changed to yellowish-green sputum.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens constantly, even during the day now. The coughing fits can be really intense, especially after I wake up in the morning or if I'm talking a lot, like with my students at school.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's about an 8 out of 10 for me. The coughing fits can be really intense and wake me up in the middle of the night, but I'm also worried because I've been taking over-the-counter dextromethorphan to try and relieve it, but it hasn't been working very well at all.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: When the coughing fits happen, I also feel like I'm having a bit of difficulty breathing and my chest feels tight. It's not just the coughing that's bothering me, but also this wheezing sound that comes with it, which is really unsettling for me.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: It definitely gets worse when I'm talking or exerting myself, like if I'm walking up stairs or trying to lift something heavy - that's when the coughing fits seem to get more intense and my breathing feels really labored.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: Actually, yes. I've been having some pretty severe night sweats for the past four nights. It's like I'm drenched in sweat by the time I wake up in the morning and need to change my pajamas because they're soaked through. That's really been alarming me, and it's making me even more concerned about what might be going on with this cough of mine.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: To be honest, I'm not really feeling much pain, but rather a deep fatigue and malaise that seems to spread throughout my whole body. The coughing fits can leave me feeling exhausted and weak, especially after they happen.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. I mean, I've been pretty active lately with work and trying to take care of myself while dealing with this cough, but I haven't noticed anything unusual about my legs or feet.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I'm taking cetirizine 10mg as needed for my seasonal allergies, and also some over-the-counter dextromethorphan for the cough. The dextromethorphan hasn't been giving me much relief, to be honest.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: I'm not sure about those specific conditions. I do know that I had a normal physical exam about a year ago, and everything seemed fine at the time. But to be honest, I haven't really thought much about my overall health in a while. Could you tell me why you're asking about those conditions?

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: To be honest, I'm not sure if I've ever had my cholesterol checked or not. I don't really recall it being mentioned during my last physical exam a year ago, and I haven't really thought about getting it checked since then. Is that something you're concerned about with this cough of mine?

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke. And as for drinking alcohol, I do have a glass or two of wine on weekends, but it's not very often and never more than that.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient:
I'd say my diet is pretty normal, I eat what most people do - a mix of fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and lean proteins. I'm not really sure if there's anything specific that could be contributing to this cough of mine, but I have been trying to eat healthier lately, especially since I've been feeling so exhausted with it.

As for exercise, I try to stay active by walking my dog every morning and doing some light yoga on the weekends. But since getting this cough, I've definitely had to take it easier - even just walking up a flight of stairs can leave me breathless and coughing really badly.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: My mother has asthma, but aside from that, I don't think there's any history of heart disease in my immediate family.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My grandmother had emphysema, and she passed away from complications related to it. But aside from that, I'm not aware of any other serious medical conditions in my immediate or extended family.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: I think it was about a year ago for my last physical exam. Everything seemed normal back then, but I don't recall getting a specific check-up since then unless something came up at work or with my family that made me need to see someone. But nothing like this cough has ever happened before, so I'm not sure if I've been due for another check-up or not.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: Yes, I do have an allergy to penicillin. When I took it in the past, it caused a rash on my skin. I try to avoid taking anything with penicillin in it, and that's why I take cetirizine for my allergies instead of something else.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: Actually, yes. As I was thinking about everything we've talked about so far, it occurred to me that I have had some wheezing episodes since I was a kid - around age 8 or 9, I'd say they started happening pretty regularly with colds and exercise. They never really got severe enough for me to see a doctor about it, but I do remember having these occasional wheezy episodes over the years, especially during cold season. It's just been something that I've learned to live with, but now that this cough has come up, I'm starting to wonder if there might be some connection between the two.
